subject: Invoice #INV-4821 – Payment Overdue (15 days)
body:
Dear Finance Team,

This is a reminder that Invoice #INV-4821 for $2,340.00 issued on March 24, 2026 is now 15 days past due. Please arrange payment immediately or contact us to discuss a payment plan. Late fees will apply after 30 days.

Best regards,
Accounts Payable
North Star Logistics

subject: ⚠️ Your account has been compromised – verify immediately
body:
URGENT: We detected suspicious login activity on your account. Click the link below immediately to verify your identity and prevent permanent account suspension:

https://securelogin-verification.net/verify?token=abc123xYZ

Failure to act within 24 hours will result in account closure. This is your only warning.

subject: Q2 All-Hands – scheduling poll
body:
Hi team,

I'm trying to schedule our Q2 all-hands for next week. Could everyone reply with their availability for Tuesday and Wednesday afternoon? We'll need about 2 hours. Please respond by Thursday EOD so I can send a calendar invite.

Thanks,
Priya

subject: Re: Ticket #8823 – Export still broken, blocking month-end
body:
Hello,

Following up on ticket #8823 from two days ago. The export button in the analytics dashboard still produces a blank PDF. This is completely blocking our month-end reporting cycle — our CFO is waiting on these numbers.

We have tried Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. Nothing works. Please escalate immediately.

Regards,
Jordan Lee
Data Analyst, ClientCo

subject: Enterprise pricing inquiry – 200-seat team
body:
Hi,

I'm the Head of Operations at Zenix Corp. We're evaluating productivity tools for our operations team (approximately 200 users) and your product came up as a top candidate.

Could we schedule a demo and discuss enterprise pricing, volume discounts, and SSO/SAML support? We're aiming to make a decision by end of Q2.

Best,
Taylor Okonkwo
Head of Operations, Zenix Corp

subject: Congratulations! You have won $1,500,000 – claim your prize
body:
Dear Lucky Winner,

You have been randomly selected to receive USD $1,500,000 from the International Digital Lottery Foundation. To claim your prize, send your full name, address, bank account details, and a small processing fee of $150 to claim@lottery-international.com.

This offer expires in 48 hours. Do not miss this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity!

subject: Office supplies reorder – Q2 budget approval needed
body:
Hi,

We're running low on several office essentials: copy paper, toner cartridges, and coffee pods. Attached is the Q2 supply list totaling $320. Please approve so I can place the order before Friday — we'll run out of paper by next week.

Thanks,
Omar

subject: Subscription renewal notice – Team Pro plan renewing April 15
body:
Hi there,

Your Team Pro subscription renews on April 15, 2026. Your card on file ending in 4242 will be charged $599/month. If you'd like to upgrade to annual billing (save 20%), switch plans, or update payment details, please do so before the renewal date.

Thank you,
Stripe Billing

subject: Scheduled DB maintenance this Saturday 2–6am UTC – brief downtime expected
body:
Team,

Ops will be performing scheduled maintenance on the primary database server this Saturday, April 12, from 2:00am–6:00am UTC. Expect brief service interruptions during that window. No user-facing action is required from your side.

I will send an all-clear message once maintenance is complete.

– Luna, Infrastructure
_routing_note: Ops/infrastructure internal emails route to support, not hr. The sender is @company.internal and the content is about server maintenance.

subject: Board meeting rescheduled – confirmed for April 17 at 2pm EST
body:
Hi,

This is to confirm that the board meeting originally scheduled for April 10 has been moved to April 17, 2026 at 2:00 PM EST due to a scheduling conflict. Please update your calendar accordingly. Secure dial-in details and the updated agenda will follow by Friday.

Kind regards,
Executive Office
Global Health Consortium

subject: ACTION REQUIRED: Your payment method has been declined
body:
Your recent payment of $599.00 has FAILED. Your account will be suspended within 24 hours unless you update your billing information immediately.

Please update your payment method at the link below to avoid service interruption:
https://stripe-payment-security.net/update?acct=4242&token=xZ9mK

If you have already updated your information, please disregard this message. Contact us at billing-support@stripe-payment-security.net for assistance.
_routing_note: Phishing email disguised as a Stripe payment failure notice. The sender domain 'stripe-payment-security.net' is not the real stripe.com. Body mentions 'payment', 'billing', '$599' which score higher than the single spam signal ('account will be suspended'), so a naive keyword classifier labels it billing. Only a domain-aware or reasoning agent catches it.

subject: ALERT: Suspicious external login attempt on company SSO – action required
body:
Team,

Our monitoring system flagged an unusual login attempt on the company SSO portal from an unrecognized IP address (185.220.101.42) at 3:47am UTC today. The attempt was blocked by MFA, but we're treating this as a potential credential stuffing attack.

Action required: All staff should review their active sessions and change passwords if anything looks unfamiliar. Do not click any unexpected login verification emails.

Security team is actively investigating. More details to follow.

– Security, company.internal
_routing_note: Internal security alert from @company.internal. 'Suspicious' and 'login' might trigger spam heuristics, but the sender is trusted internal. Internal security emails route to security team, not hr.

subject: Bulk export timing out + question about API access pricing
body:
Hi support,

We have an unresolved bug with bulk data export — CSV exports consistently time out for datasets over 50,000 rows. I submitted a ticket about this last week but haven't heard back.

Separately (not urgent): could you forward me the pricing for API access? If this export bug can't be fixed quickly, we may want to build our own integration via the API as a workaround.

The bug is the main issue here — it's blocking our daily reporting pipeline.

Thanks,
Wei Zhang, ClientCo
_routing_note: Mixed-signal email: mentions pricing (sales signal) but the primary issue is a bug report that is 'blocking our daily reporting pipeline'. Support intent dominates; priority is high because the export outage is actively blocking production work.

subject: Action required: Your NDA is awaiting signature – expires in 24 hours
body:
Hello,

A document has been shared with you for review and signing via DocuSign.

Document: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (MNDA-2026-Q2)
Sent by: legal@partnerco.com
Expires: April 12, 2026

Please review and sign the document at your earliest convenience:
https://docusign-esignature.net/sign?doc=MNDA-2026&token=x8Kp2mZ

If you have any questions about this document, please contact the sender directly.

Powered by DocuSign
docusign-esignature.net
_routing_note: Phishing email impersonating DocuSign. The real domain is docusign.com — 'docusign-esignature.net' is a lookalike. The body contains no billing or classic spam keywords (no 'winner', 'lottery', 'bank account details'), using only document/signature/legal language that evades keyword classifiers. Only a domain-aware or reasoning agent catches it as phishing.

subject: 🎉 We WON the Gartner Cool Vendor award – free team dinner this Friday!
body:
Hi everyone,

Exciting news — InboxOps has been named a Gartner Cool Vendor for 2026! 🎉

To celebrate, I'm treating the entire team to a free dinner this Friday at 7pm at The Ivy. Details and dietary preference form to follow by tomorrow.

This is a huge milestone and I couldn't be more proud of what we've built together. Bring your partner if you'd like.

See you Friday!

Maya Patel
CEO, InboxOps
_routing_note: Legitimate internal email from the CEO (@company.internal). The subject contains 'WON' and 'free' — classic spam trigger words — but the sender is a trusted internal address and the content is a genuine team celebration. A keyword classifier mislabels it as spam/security; an LLM recognises the internal sender and context.

subject: API rate limit errors blocking our pipeline – looking at Enterprise tier
body:
Hi,

We've been hitting rate limit errors on your API constantly for the past two weeks. I opened a ticket (#9041) about it but haven't heard back.

I understand we're on the Starter plan (1,000 requests/hour). Our production pipeline now requires roughly 8,000 requests/hour and the current limit is causing failures every day.

Honestly, I think we've outgrown the Starter plan. I'd like to get pricing for the Enterprise tier — specifically for 50 seats with a higher API rate limit and SLA guarantees. Could someone from your sales or partnerships team reach out this week?

Priya Raghavan
Head of Data, ScaleTech
_routing_note: The surface-level signals (rate limit errors, ticket, pipeline failures) look like a support request, and a keyword classifier routes it there. But the primary ask is explicit: 'I'd like to get pricing for the Enterprise tier... Could someone from your sales team reach out?' An LLM understands the intent is an upgrade/sales inquiry, not a bug report.

subject: Important: Your April salary payment details have been updated
body:
Dear Employee,

We have migrated to a new payroll processing system. To ensure your April salary payment is deposited to the correct account, please confirm your payment details at the secure link below:

https://direct-deposit-update.com/confirm?emp=4829&token=zR7wX

Please action this by April 10 to avoid any delay to your monthly payment.

HR & Payroll Administration
_routing_note: Payroll phishing from the fake domain 'direct-deposit-update.com'. The body uses 'payment' and 'salary payment' which score as billing keywords, so a keyword classifier labels it billing/finance. The fake domain (no legitimate company sends payroll from 'direct-deposit-update.com') and the credential-harvesting pattern are what identify it as phishing — requiring domain-aware reasoning.

subject: New sign-in to your GitHub organisation from an unrecognised device
body:
Hi,

We noticed a new sign-in to your GitHub organisation 'inboxops-eng' from a device we don't recognise.

Device: Windows 11 · Chrome 124
Location: Frankfurt, Germany
Time: April 11, 2026 at 06:14 UTC

If this was you, no action is needed. If you do not recognise this activity, we strongly recommend reviewing your organisation's member access and revoking any unfamiliar sessions at github.com/orgs/inboxops-eng/settings/security.

For help, visit GitHub Support.

The GitHub Team
github.com
_routing_note: Legitimate security alert from the real github.com domain about potentially unauthorised access to the company's GitHub organisation — a high-priority internal security matter. A keyword classifier assigns the wrong owner (hr instead of security) and wrong priority (medium instead of high) because it relies on exact keyword matches like 'security team' or 'sso portal' which don't appear. An LLM understands this is an IT security event that must reach the security team immediately.
